name: NetSuite Finance Demo: Standard Reporting and Drilldown

avoid: custom saved reports for prospect demos
deep links unless used as fallback after a learned route
navigationPolicy.reportPolicy: Use standard NetSuite reports for prospect-facing demos unless the user explicitly asks for a custom report.

login.instructions: Open the browser window, sign in to NetSuite, and leave it on any authenticated page. The runner waits for the global Search box before starting.

demoRequest.topic: Generate a finance demo from the pre-demo notes. Focus on standard income statement and profitability reporting, drilldown from summary to transaction detail, export options and fewer spreadsheet handoffs and subsidiary and consolidated finance views. Demo scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..
demoRequest.demoScope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management.
demoRequest.competition: Known competition/status quo: SAP S/4HANA and Microsoft Dynamics are being considered alongside the current architecture of Access ERP, Jedox consolidation/planning, spreadsheets, email-heavy AP, and Payhawk/Access Expense in parts of the business.
demoRequest.manifestDemoModeInstruction: Turn the manifest into a customer-centered story. Use the selected audience and target audience playbook to personalize narration, proof moments, and the SC runbook.
demoRequest.demoStrategyInstruction: Demo strategy: Standard Platform Demo. Keep the tone polished, structured, and business-focused, pacing balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first, technical depth light to moderate unless requested, and story style broad end-to-end platform story connecting multiple stakeholder perspectives. Prioritize end-to-end business flows, cross-functional workflows, executive dashboards, reporting and analytics and automation examples. Behavior rules: prioritize breadth over deep specialization, ensure all major departments are represented, maintain consistent pacing, avoid excessive technical depth unless requested and make executive-level value visible throughout. Adapt the examples to General Business using finance visibility, operational control and growth readiness.
demoRequest.industryInstruction: Industry playbook: General Business. Use terminology such as finance visibility, operational control and growth readiness, emphasize KPIs such as cash position, profitability, close speed and forecast accuracy, and avoid industry-specific claims without discovery and unsupported benchmark statements.
demoRequest.outputLanguageInstruction: Write SC-facing output, setup guidance, and narrator talk tracks in English.
demoRequest.audienceInstruction: Demo to the Prospect audience type in a Mid-Market target audience context. Potential buyers evaluating whether the solution solves their business challenges and delivers measurable value. Focus on business value, ROI, ease of adoption and competitive differentiation. Include business pain points, before/after scenarios, success stories and metrics and outcomes. Avoid deep technical configuration, feature dumping, internal terminology and long setup explanations. For Mid-Market, use a process-oriented, business-value focused and real-world operational scenarios demo style. Prioritize process efficiency, visibility, integration and cross-team collaboration; include role-based workflows, analytics dashboards, integrations and approval processes; avoid oversimplified startup messaging, feature dumping, extremely technical implementation details and unnecessary enterprise governance complexity.
demoRequest.instructions:
Lead with the customer's business problem and desired outcome before showing screens.
Open with the biggest business hitters first: executive visibility, trusted numbers, consolidation confidence, close speed, project profitability, auditability, approvals, and fewer spreadsheets.
Use a crisp tell-show-tell rhythm: frame the point, show the shortest proof path, then land why it matters in plain language.
Use NetSuite global search and the navigation bar as the primary way to move through the product.
Use standard NetSuite reports for prospect-facing finance demos unless the audience explicitly asks for custom reporting.
Choose the optimal demo flow and put the highest-impact moments first.
Start with a general executive overview of NetSuite before moving into the specific workflow.
Keep SuiteProjects and Fixed Asset Management within the agreed scope, but do not let them overshadow the primary financials story.
Avoid feature tours, menu wandering, slow setup, dead clicks, and deep configuration unless intentionally requested.
Never announce internal labels such as value statement during narration.

additionalDemoSources.label: Use Standard NetSuite Reports First
additionalDemoSources.content: For prospect-facing finance demos, prioritize standard NetSuite reports and dashboards before custom reporting or saved-search detail.
additionalDemoSources.guidance: Use this as a routing and narration rule when the demo touches financial reporting, P&L, drilldown, export, or finance visibility.
additionalDemoSources.logic: If the demo asks for reporting or finance visibility, keep standard reports in the primary path and park custom reporting as an optional follow-up.

demoRequest.instruction: Use NetSuite navigation/search first, use standard reports for prospect-facing demos, and keep custom report links only as explicit fallbacks. Start with a short general or executive NetSuite overview, then order the demo from highest-value proof moments to supporting detail. Demo scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management. Competition/status quo context to consider: Known competition/status quo: SAP S/4HANA and Microsoft Dynamics are being considered alongside the current architecture of Access ERP, Jedox consolidation/planning, spreadsheets, email-heavy AP, and Payhawk/Access Expense in parts of the business.. Avoid deep technical configuration, feature dumping, internal terminology and long setup explanations. For Mid-Market, use a process-oriented, business-value focused and real-world operational scenarios demo style. Prioritize process efficiency, visibility, integration and cross-team collaboration; include role-based workflows, analytics dashboards, integrations and approval processes; avoid oversimplified startup messaging, feature dumping, extremely technical implementation details and unnecessary enterprise governance complexity. Keep the tone polished, structured, and business-focused, pacing balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first, technical depth light to moderate unless requested, and story style broad end-to-end platform story connecting multiple stakeholder perspectives. Prioritize end-to-end business flows, cross-functional workflows, executive dashboards, reporting and analytics and automation examples. codexPrepAnalysis.analysis:
# Codex Prep Operator Analysis
Task title: created by demo helper - Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters

## Company Context That Should Shape The Demo
- Air Charter Service positions itself as a global aircraft charter broker across private jets, group charters, and cargo, not as the aircraft operator. Source: https://www.aircharterservice.com/
- Public site scale is material: 35,000+ flights per year, 40+ offices across 6 continents, and access to 50,000 aircraft. This supports a multi-entity, high-volume, globally distributed finance story.
- Their business is bespoke/job-based: each charter has customer, route, aircraft, timing, supplier/operator cost, supporting documentation, and margin implications.
- The site highlights private, group, cargo, leasing, time-critical, onboard courier, trucking, and customs-adjacent services, so reporting needs to cut across business lines, geographies, and service types.
- Recent ACS news says turnover reached US$1.34B for the year ended January 31, 2026, with growth across divisions. Source: https://www.aircharterservice.com/about-us/news-features/news/air-charter-services-revenues-grow-by-more-than-10-percent-to-1-point-34-billion-us-dollars
- Cargo and urgent logistics add complexity around fast-moving supplier costs, documentation, recharges, and exception handling. Source: https://www.aircharterservice.com/cargo-charter/

## Likely ERP Priorities
- Consolidated, trusted financial reporting across countries, entities, currencies, and local accounting requirements.
- Executive visibility into income statement, profitability, cash exposure, AP/AR, and entity performance without waiting for spreadsheet consolidation.
- Drilldown from consolidated P&L to subsidiary, account, transaction, vendor bill, invoice, and supporting record.
- Flight or booking profitability by project, including revenue, supplier/operator costs, broker/office ownership, business type, and route attributes.
- Faster close with fewer manual reconciliations between ERP, Jedox, Payhawk, Access Expense, CRM, and spreadsheets.
- Approval workflows that give budget holders and finance approvers enough context to approve vendor bills, expenses, and payments confidently.
- Auditability, role separation, and transaction traceability for AR, AP, finance operations, and controller review.
- Fixed asset control for office equipment and intangibles, replacing spreadsheet depreciation tracking.

## Recommended Demo Direction
Start with the business problem: ACS needs finance leadership to trust consolidated numbers across a global, high-volume charter operation while still being able to explain margin on an individual flight or booking.

Open with a short executive NetSuite overview: one system of record, role-based finance home, global search, standard reports, drilldown, approvals, audit trail, and export where needed. Then go straight into the biggest proof moments:

1. Consolidated income statement: show standard financial reporting at group level, then switch subsidiary/entity view to prove consolidation confidence.
2. Drilldown: move from P&L line to account detail to transaction detail, showing how finance can validate numbers without spreadsheet handoffs.
3. Profitability by flight/project: use SuiteProjects to show a booking/flight with revenue, vendor bills/costs, owner/broker/office dimensions, and margin.
4. Approval context: show a vendor bill or expense approval tied to a flight/project with documents and approval status.
5. Close/audit/export: show audit trail, transaction history, and export options as controlled outputs, not as the primary reporting engine.
6. Fixed assets: keep brief; show asset register and depreciation as a finance control point, not the center of the story.

## Scope And Account Prep Implications
- Demo account should have OneWorld-style subsidiary structure with at least group/consolidated view plus sample entities in different regions.
- Chart of accounts should support standard income statement and balance sheet reporting, with realistic revenue and cost categories for private, group, cargo, leasing/time-critical services.
- Use dimensions such as subsidiary, location/office, department, class/business type, customer, broker/owner, and project/flight.
- Create several flight/booking projects with route, aircraft type, start/end date, customer, broker, revenue, vendor/operator costs, and margin.
- Add sample AR invoices, vendor bills, credit notes, approvals, and attached supporting documents.
- Include at least one cross-border example, such as revenue/cost ownership split across two offices or entities.
- Prepare fixed asset examples for office equipment and intangible assets with straight-line depreciation.
- Keep standard NetSuite reports ready: income statement, balance sheet, transaction detail, AP/AR aging, project profitability, and subsidiary/consolidated views.

## Discovery Gaps That Could Hurt The Demo
- Confirm active legal entities, countries, base currencies, and consolidation hierarchy.
- Clarify local GAAP, statutory reporting, and whether Multi-Book is required in scope or only a future consideration.
- Confirm which countries send and receive e-invoices, and which remain with third-party providers.
- Confirm required reporting dimensions: legal entity, office, business type, broker, customer, route, aircraft type, flight/project, and operational owner.
- Clarify whether Veluxis aircraft management is part of this demo story or a separate business model.
- Confirm how IBOS CRM, Payhawk, Access Expense, Jedox, and ODBC reporting should be positioned: integrate now, phase 2, or acknowledge only.
- Confirm approval matrix details: thresholds, budget holders, delegated authority, mobile approval expectations, and document requirements.
- Confirm whether FP&A/budgeting should be shown only as standard budget reporting or parked for a later planning demo.
codexPrepAnalysis.sessionTitle: created by demo helper - Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters

context.preDemoNotes:
# Discovery & Demo Preparation Notes

## Current Scope & Discussion Areas

* Procure-to-Pay Phase 2 discussion
* Number of Payhawk expense users?
* Budget holders and approval workflows
* FP&A requirements
* Advanced Inventory requirements
* Multi-Book accounting considerations
* ACS considerations
* Role separation between AR/AP teams and finance operations

---

# Key Stakeholders

| Name        | Role                           | Notes                                                   |
| ----------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Paul Miles  | Project Manager                | Overall project coordination                            |
| Henry Davis | Business Analyst               | Business process analysis                               |
| Rob         | IT Director                    | Responsible for technical architecture and integrations |
| Jo          | Head of IT Operations          | Responsible for production operations                   |
| Jonathan    | Financial Controller           | Core finance stakeholder                                |
| Tom         | Finance Transformation Manager | Previously Financial Controller                         |
| Cary        | Group CFO                      | Executive sponsor / financial leadership                |

---

# Project Context

The customer initially explored a standalone Procure-to-Pay initiative but has since expanded the conversation into a broader ERP transformation project.

The organization operates across multiple countries and legal entities, creating significant consolidation and reporting complexity.

---

# Current Systems & Processes

## Expense Management

* Payhawk is currently used for part of the business expense process
* Not used for the core air charter business
* Some entities still rely on Access Expense or manual/paper-based processes

## Consolidation & Reporting

Current challenges:

* Group consolidations
* Multi-country reporting
* UK-centric ERP limitations
* Local GAAP chart of accounts requirements
* Country-specific tax reporting
* E-invoicing complexity
* Difficulty scaling into new regions/entities

Consolidation is currently performed in Jedox due to limitations in the existing ERP system.

Important positioning opportunity:

* NetSuite OneWorld and native consolidation capabilities should be highlighted strongly during the demo.

---

# International & Localization Requirements

Need clarity on:

* Active countries/legal entities
* Language requirements by country
* Dual-language requirements in some regions
* Country-specific tax and e-invoicing regulations

Important discovery question:

> Which countries are currently sending and/or receiving e-invoices?

---

# Integration Challenges

Integration complexity is a major concern.

Key focus areas:

* Existing architecture compatibility
* Integration flexibility
* ODBC/reporting requirements
* Data flow between systems
* CRM integration strategy

Current CRM:

* IBOS CRM
* Potential future replacement opportunity
* Current scope remains finance-focused

Likely integration requirements:

* Booking references
* Credit notes
* Flight/project data synchronization

---

# Core Business Process - Flight Project Tracking

Each flight is treated as a unique project.

Key project tracking requirements:

* Flight start/end date
* Route information
* Aircraft type
* Broker involved
* Customer information
* Associated transactions
* Revenue and cost tracking
* Operational ownership

The customer wants complete financial visibility tied directly to each flight/project.

---

# Aircraft Management Business Model

Veluxis operates as a private jet management company.

Business model:

* Wealthy individuals/companies own aircraft
* Veluxis manages operations on their behalf
* Veluxis arranges:

  * maintenance
  * charter flights
  * operational coordination

Operational complexity:

* Costs are incurred by Veluxis
* Costs are then recharged/disbursed to aircraft owners
* Current handling relies heavily on separate balance sheet accounts

Potential demo focus:

* Intercompany/accounting visibility
* Project profitability
* Cost allocation transparency

---

# Procure-to-Pay & AP Challenges

Current process is highly manual and email-driven.

Current state:

* No formal PO process
* Each booking/flight is bespoke
* Brokers send invoices from airlines
* Heavy reliance on email communication
* Manual payment coordination
* Limited centralized visibility

Potential opportunity areas:

* Approval workflows
* Vendor bill automation
* Centralized document management
* Workflow tracking
* Payment visibility

---

# Accounts Receivable & Dunning

The organization can report effectively on outstanding balances.

Main challenge:

* Delayed customer payments often caused by incomplete or incorrect supporting documentation
* No centralized storage for customer-facing information

Potential positioning:

* Document management
* Transaction visibility
* Automated communication
* Customer record centralization

---

# Revenue Recognition

Revenue recognition occurs:

* On the date/month the flight takes place

Revenue must be tracked by:

* Legal entity
* Office/location
* Business type

Examples:

* Cargo flights
* Private jets
* Passenger flights

Complexity example:

* Revenue may involve both Dubai and India operations simultaneously

Need visibility into:

* Entity ownership
* Department allocation
* Cross-border operational reporting

---

# FP&A & Budgeting

Current state:

* FP&A primarily handled in Jedox
* Additional budgeting work handled in spreadsheets
* Budgets maintained globally in GBP
* Budget data is not written back into ERP dimensions

Future opportunity:

* FP&A modernization
* Planning and budgeting centralization
* Expanded reporting visibility

Potential Phase 2 discussion:

* Native planning/budgeting capabilities

---

# Fixed Assets & Expense Deferrals

## Fixed Assets

Current process:

* Spreadsheets
* Straight-line depreciation
* Office equipment
* Intangible assets

## Expense Deferrals

* Limited use currently
* Primarily bill-related

---

# Reporting & Analytics

ODBC access is important.

However:

* Many required reports appear achievable with standard NetSuite reporting

Important demo guidance:

* Strongly highlight out-of-the-box reporting capabilities
* Avoid over-positioning custom reports unless truly necessary

---

# Templates & Document Configuration

Customer sends templates externally.

Demo should include:

* Template configuration capabilities
* Document customization
* Customer-facing output flexibility

---

# E-Invoicing

Current approach:

* Third-party provider handles many e-invoicing submissions

Some countries may benefit from direct handling internally, while others prefer outsourcing.

Need clarity on:

* Sending vs receiving requirements
* Country-by-country obligations
* Integration expectations

---

# Approval Workflows

Approval matrix already exists with thresholds and hierarchies.

Current challenge:

* Approvers often lack visibility/context into what they are approving

Potential demo focus:

* Approval visibility
* Supporting documentation
* Workflow clarity
* Context-aware approvals
* Mobile approvals

demoStrategy.description: A broad, structured product demonstration that showcases the overall platform, major capabilities, and end-to-end business value within a fixed timeframe.
demoStrategy.pacing: balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first

avoid excessive technical depth unless requested
connect workflows through one narrative story where possible

setupPlan.accountCreateWarning: Creating records or configuration in NetSuite can affect the connected account. Always confirm the exact account, role, and records before executing.

items.label: sample customer

items.label: sample vendor
items.reason: needed if the story should show payables, bills, or supplier exposure

items.label: sample item
items.reason: needed if operational transactions require item detail

items.label: sample customer invoice
items.reason: needed to demonstrate receivables, collections, and cash inflow

items.label: sample vendor bill
items.reason: needed to demonstrate payables, approvals, and cash outflow

items.label: sample purchase order
items.reason: needed to demonstrate future outflow or procure-to-pay context

items.label: subsidiary or entity demo context
items.reason: needed to demonstrate multi-entity filtering or consolidation

items.label: role/permission or approval setup
items.reason: needed if the story requires controls, approvals, or role-based access

items.label: fixed assets demo context
items.reason: needed if fixed assets are in scope for the personalized story or setup prompt

items.label: planning and budgeting demo context
items.reason: needed if FP&A, budgets, or planning are in scope for the demo

items.label: services SKU or project demo context
items.reason: needed if the scoped demo should show services-led financials or project profitability

- Confirm required reporting dimensions: legal entity, office, business type, broker, customer, route, aircraft type, flight/project, and operational owner.
- Clarify whether Veluxis aircraft management is part of this demo story or a separate business model.
- Confirm how IBOS CRM, Payhawk, Access Expense, Jedox, and ODBC reporting should be positioned: integrate now, phase 2, or acknowledge only.
- Confirm approval matrix details: thresholds, budget holders, delegated authority, mobile approval expectations, and document requirements.
- Confirm whether FP&A/budgeting should be shown only as standard budget reporting or parked for a later planning demo.
codexPrepAnalysis.sessionTitle: created by demo helper - Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters

context.demoScope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management.

additionalDemoSources.label: Use Standard NetSuite Reports First
additionalDemoSources.content: For prospect-facing finance demos, prioritize standard NetSuite reports and dashboards before custom reporting or saved-search detail.
additionalDemoSources.guidance: Use this as a routing and narration rule when the demo touches financial reporting, P&L, drilldown, export, or finance visibility.
additionalDemoSources.logic: If the demo asks for reporting or finance visibility, keep standard reports in the primary path and park custom reporting as an optional follow-up.

demoPrep.scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management.
demoPrep.opening: Start with a short general or executive NetSuite overview before the first detailed workflow.
demoPrep.ordering: Order the demo from strongest business impact to supporting detail: executive overview, trusted reporting, filters, drilldown, controlled sharing, the next in-scope proof point, then close.
demoPrep.scopeInstruction: Stay inside this demo scope unless the SC deliberately branches: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..
demoPrep.audienceInstruction: Demo to the Prospect audience type in a Mid-Market target audience context. Potential buyers evaluating whether the solution solves their business challenges and delivers measurable value. Focus on business value, ROI, ease of adoption and competitive differentiation. Include business pain points, before/after scenarios, success stories and metrics and outcomes. Avoid deep technical configuration, feature dumping, internal terminology and long setup explanations. For Mid-Market, use a process-oriented, business-value focused and real-world operational scenarios demo style. Prioritize process efficiency, visibility, integration and cross-team collaboration; include role-based workflows, analytics dashboards, integrations and approval processes; avoid oversimplified startup messaging, feature dumping, extremely technical implementation details and unnecessary enterprise governance complexity.
demoPrep.strategyInstruction: Demo strategy: Standard Platform Demo. Keep the tone polished, structured, and business-focused, pacing balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first, technical depth light to moderate unless requested, and story style broad end-to-end platform story connecting multiple stakeholder perspectives. Prioritize end-to-end business flows, cross-functional workflows, executive dashboards, reporting and analytics and automation examples. Behavior rules: prioritize breadth over deep specialization, ensure all major departments are represented, maintain consistent pacing, avoid excessive technical depth unless requested and make executive-level value visible throughout. Adapt the examples to General Business using finance visibility, operational control and growth readiness.
principles: Start with the general NetSuite story and why the platform matters to leadership.
Show the highest-value proof moments before lower-level configuration or optional detail.
Use the stated demo scope as a hard planning input even when the notes do not mention it.
Keep standard NetSuite reports and navigation in the main path.
Defer lower-value or out-of-scope topics to Q&A or appendix.

manifestBuild.instruction: Live demo functionality is switched off. The SC guide and intelligence outputs are ready; no dry-run creation prompt or runnable manifest is generated.

dryRunCreationPrompt.instruction: This timestamp confirms when the SC Guide tab's dry-run creation prompt was last prepared for manifest generation.

context.competition: Known competition/status quo: SAP S/4HANA and Microsoft Dynamics are being considered alongside the current architecture of Access ERP, Jedox consolidation/planning, spreadsheets, email-heavy AP, and Payhawk/Access Expense in parts of the business.

slider.label: Light
slider.description: Add value points at the big transitions only.

slider.label: Balanced

slider.label: Heavy

runButtons.label: Dry Run
runButtons.description: Print the planned demo without opening NetSuite.

runButtons.label: Rehearse And Prep Account

runButtons.label: Run Live Demo
runButtons.description: Run the browser automation with narration.

segments.title: Frame The Executive NetSuite Overview
segments.objective: Set up the general NetSuite platform story before opening detailed finance workflows.
segments.valueStatement: Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters first sees why NetSuite matters at the executive level: visibility, control, trusted numbers, and a clear path from summary to action.
segments.narration: Before we open the first report, frame the executive view for Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters: NetSuite is the operating system where finance can see performance, prove the detail, and make decisions from one controlled place. We will start with the highest-value proof moments, then move into supporting detail. Keep the live path inside this scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..

actions.text: Executive overview. Audience: Prospect. Target: Mid-Market. Strategy: Standard Platform Demo. Industry: General Business. Stay inside this demo scope unless the SC deliberately branches: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..

segments.title: Open The Standard Income Statement
segments.objective: Use NetSuite search/navigation to open a standard profitability report.
segments.valueStatement: Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters gets a fast, trusted performance view that connects directly to trusted reporting and drilldown from summary to detail.
segments.narration: For Prospect, keep the narration persuasive, outcome-oriented and story-driven and anchored in business value, ROI, ease of adoption and competitive differentiation. For Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters, let's start where a finance leader usually wants to start: the standard income statement. This gives a quick view of revenue, margin, overheads, and net profit from one controlled place in NetSuite.

segments.title: Read The Finance Story
segments.objective: Show the CFO-level view: sales, gross profit, operating profit, and net result.
segments.valueStatement: A first-time viewer can understand performance without needing to know where every transaction lives.
segments.narration: For someone new to the system, this report is intentionally familiar. Turnover is at the top, overheads sit below it, and operating profit and net profit are easy to find at the bottom.

segments.title: Show Report Filters
segments.objective: Highlight the filter set finance can use to re-slice the result.
segments.valueStatement: Private, Group & Cargo Air Charters can re-run the same standard report by period, subsidiary, and accounting book, which supports trusted reporting and drilldown from summary to detail.
segments.narration: Now let's look at how a team can change the lens. In the standard report, the most important controls are period, date range, subsidiary context, and accounting book.

segments.title: Drill Into The Numbers
segments.objective: Move from summary P&L to the transaction-level support behind a number.
segments.valueStatement: When a number is questioned, the team can investigate it from the report instead of starting a separate reconciliation exercise.
segments.narration: If someone asks what sits behind a number, we can drill from the summary into the supporting detail. That turns the report from a static total into an explainable result.
fallback: If the drilldown route changes, click a blue linked report amount or account line such as Revenue, then return to the saved report.

segments.title: Show Export Options
segments.objective: Show how the report can be shared or taken offline.
segments.valueStatement: Teams can keep NetSuite as the source of truth while still producing the files people expect for reviews, audits, and board packs.
segments.narration: When the report needs to leave the system, the export options are already here. Excel, PDF, CSV, Word, and print are available without rebuilding the report somewhere else.
